extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout and LAPORTE Christophe rode away from 20-rider group after Jumbo-Visma took top three positions into Patterberg, van Aert won with 36km solo.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma dominated Patterberg entry with all three leadout positions, launching dual attack that no team could counter organizationally.
Van Aert/Laporte gap jumped from 15 to 20 seconds within two climbs post-Patterberg, suggesting chase hesitation from mixed-team composition.
Ineos chase work brought peloton close enough for Pidcock bridge at Eikenberg, revealing their willingness to burn matches early.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Second teammate duo breakaway win this season suggests Jumbo's numeric superiority renders traditional chase dynamics obsolete
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: Survived Patterberg selection and sustained power over 36km indicates climbing weight has not compromised cobbled strength
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: Fifth place at age 21 in debut cobbled classic suggests Eritrean can handle Northern classics, not just sprints
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Jonathan: neutral: Required Ineos team support to bridge at Eikenberg rather than solo effort, questioning his diesel engine claims
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: negative: Defending champion dropped on Patterberg and finished ninth, form regression from 2021 concerning for Quick-Step
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: positive: Solo third place from chase group shows his move timing exploited hesitation from sprint-cautious chasers
